sources.name: App
sources.snippet:
function App() {
  const [isReady, setIsReady] = useState(false)

  useEffect(() => {
    void Promise.all([initPersistedState(), Geo.resolve(), setupDeviceId]).then(
      () => setIsReady(true),
    )
  }, [])

  if (!isReady) {
    return null
  }

  /*
   * NOTE: only nothing here can depend on other data or session state, since
   * that is set up in the InnerApp component above.
   */
  return (
    <Geo.Provider>
      <AppConfigProvider>
        <A11yProvider>
          <KeyboardControllerProvider>
            <OnboardingProvider>
              <AnalyticsContext>
                <SessionProvider>
                  <PrefsStateProvider>
                    <I18nProvider>
                      <ShellStateProvider>
                        <DialogStateProvider>
                          <LightboxStateProvider>
                            <PortalProvider>
                              <LandingProvider>
                                <InnerApp />
                              </LandingProvider>
                            </PortalProvider>
                          </LightboxStateProvider>
                        </DialogStateProvider>
                      </ShellStateProvider>
                    </I18nProvider>
                  </PrefsStateProvider>
                </SessionProvider>
              </AnalyticsContext>
            </OnboardingProvider>
          </KeyboardControllerProvider>
        </A11yProvider>
      </AppConfigProvider>
    </Geo.Provider>
  )
}

sources.name: App
sources.snippet:
function App() {
  const [isReady, setIsReady] = useState(false)

  useEffect(() => {
    void Promise.all([initPersistedState(), Geo.resolve(), setupDeviceId]).then(
      () => setIsReady(true),
    )
  }, [])

  if (!isReady) {
    return null
  }

  /*
   * NOTE: only nothing here can depend on other data or session state, since
   * that is set up in the InnerApp component above.
   */
  return (
    <Geo.Provider>
      <AppConfigProvider>
        <A11yProvider>
          <KeyboardControllerProvider preload={false}>
            <OnboardingProvider>
              <AnalyticsContext>
                <SessionProvider>
                  <PrefsStateProvider>
                    <I18nProvider>
                      <ShellStateProvider>
                        <DialogStateProvider>
                          <LightboxStateProvider>
                            <PortalProvider>
                              <BottomSheetProvider>
                                <LandingProvider>
                                  <SafeAreaProvider
                                    initialMetrics={initialWindowMetrics}>
                                    <InnerApp />
                                  </SafeAreaProvider>
                                </LandingProvider>
                              </BottomSheetProvider>
                            </PortalProvider>
                          </LightboxStateProvider>
                        </DialogStateProvider>
                      </ShellStateProvider>
                    </I18nProvider>
                  </PrefsStateProvider>
                </SessionProvider>
              </AnalyticsContext>
            </OnboardingProvider>
          </KeyboardControllerProvider>
        </A11yProvider>
      </AppConfigProvider>
    </Geo.Provider>
  )
}

runs.question: There appear to be two different useTheme hooks in the codebase. Where is each defined?
runs.expected_answer: src/alf/index.tsx#useTheme (the alf design-system theme hook) and src/lib/ThemeContext.tsx#useTheme (the legacy ThemeContext hook).
runs.answer:
The codebase contains two distinct **`useTheme`** hooks:

| Hook name | Definition location | Line range |
|-----------|--------------------|------------|
| **`useTheme`** (the simple context hook) | `src/lib/ThemeContext.tsx` | line 92 – 92【src/lib/ThemeContext.tsx:92-92】 |
| **`useTheme`** (the Alf‑specific hook that returns a theme object from the Alf store) | `src/alf/index.tsx` | line 140 – 145【src/alf/index.tsx:140-145】 |

Thus, one `useTheme` is defined in **`ThemeContext.tsx`** as a thin wrapper around `useContext(ThemeContext)`, while the other is defined in **`alf/index.tsx`** and derives the theme from the Alf state via `useAlf` and `useMemo`.
runs.confidence_rationale: 0 of 3 retrieved entities scored >=0.6 relevance; top match scored 0.34.

sources.snippet:
export function FollowingEmptyState() {
  const {t: l} = useLingui()
  const pal = usePalette('default')
  const navigation = useNavigation<NavigationProp>()

  const onPressFindAccounts = useCallback(() => {
    if (IS_WEB) {
      navigation.navigate('Search', {})
    } else {
      navigation.navigate('SearchTab')
      navigation.popToTop()
    }
  }, [navigation])

  const onPressDiscoverFeeds = useCallback(() => {
    navigation.navigate('Feeds')
  }, [navigation])

  return (
    <View style={styles.container}>
      <View style={styles.inner}>
        <View style={styles.iconContainer}>
          <MagnifyingGlassIcon style={[styles.icon, pal.text]} size={62} />
        </View>
        <Text type="xl-medium" style={[s.textCenter, pal.text]}>
          <Trans>
            Your following feed is empty! Follow more users to see what's
            happening.
          </Trans>
        </Text>
        <View style={[a.mt_xl, a.align_center]}>
          <Button
            label={l`Find accounts to follow`}
            onPress={onPressFindAccounts}
            color="secondary_inverted"
            size="large">
            <ButtonText>
              <Trans>Find accounts to follow</Trans>
            </ButtonText>
            <ButtonIcon icon={ChevronRightIcon} />
          </Button>
        </View>

        <Text type="xl-medium" style={[s.textCenter, pal.text, s.mt20]}>
          <Trans>You can also discover new Custom Feeds to follow.</Trans>
        </Text>
        <View style={[a.mt_md, a.align_center]}>
          <Button
            label={l`Discover new custom feeds`}
            onPress={onPressDiscoverFeeds}
            color="secondary_inverted"
            size="large">
            <ButtonText>
              <Trans>Discover new custom feeds</Trans>
            </ButtonText>
            <ButtonIcon icon={ChevronRightIcon} />
          </Button>
        </View>
      </View>
    </View>
# ... truncated ...

runs.question: What breaks if the useAgent hook changes its behavior -- who calls it directly?
runs.expected_answer: Direct callers include useFeedFeedback (state/feed-feedback.tsx), useMigrateMutes (state/cache/thread-mutes.tsx), and the app-passwords query/mutation hooks in state/queries/app-passwords.ts.
runs.answer:
The **`useAgent`** hook is used directly by a handful of other hooks. If its behavior changes, any of those callers will be affected because they rely on the `AtpAgent` instance returned by `useAgent`.

**Direct callers of `useAgent`**

| Caller (hook) | File & line range |
|---------------|-------------------|
| `useFeedFeedback` | `src/state/feed-feedback.tsx:62‑251` |
| `useMigrateMutes` | `src/state/cache/thread-mutes.tsx:58‑104` |
| `useAppPasswordsQuery` | `src/state/queries/app-passwords.ts:10‑20` |
| `useAppPasswordCreateMutation` | `src/state/queries/app-passwords.ts:22‑44` |
| `useAppPasswordDeleteMutation` | `src/state/queries/app-passwords.ts:46‑61` |
| `usePinnedPostMutation` | `src/state/queries/pinned‑post.ts:12‑87` |

These six hooks invoke `useAgent` (as shown by the “Called by” edges in the code graph). Any change to `useAgent`—for example, altering the returned `AtpAgent` instance, throwing different errors, or modifying its side‑effects—could break the functionality of these hooks and, consequently, any UI components or screens that depend on them.
runs.confidence_rationale: 0 of 5 retrieved entities scored >=0.6 relevance; top match scored 0.36.

runs.question: Which screen shows the user's notifications, and which hooks does it call for unread-notification state?
runs.expected_answer: NotificationsScreen in src/view/screens/Notifications.tsx, which calls useUnreadNotifications and useUnreadNotificationsApi from state/queries/notifications/unread.
runs.answer:
The notifications UI is rendered by **`NotificationsScreen`** (defined in `src/view/screens/Notifications.tsx` lines 49‑170) 【source:src/view/screens/Notifications.tsx:49-170】.  

For the unread‑notification state the screen (and the components it composes) rely on the hooks defined in the notifications query module:

* **`useUnreadNotifications`** – returns the unread‑notifications context 【source:src/state/queries/notifications/unread.tsx#useUnreadNotifications:220-222】  
* **`useUnreadNotificationsApi`** – returns the API helpers for checking unread notifications 【source:src/state/queries/notifications/unread.tsx#useUnreadNotificationsApi:224-226】

These two hooks provide the current unread‑notification count and the function to sync/check it with the server, and are used by the notifications screen to display the user's notifications and indicate unread items.

sources.name: feed.ts
sources.snippet:
/**
 * NOTE
 * The ./unread.ts API:
 *
 * - Provides a `checkUnread()` function to sync with the server,
 * - Periodically calls `checkUnread()`, and
 * - Caches the first page of notifications.
 *
 * IMPORTANT: This query uses ./unread.ts's cache as its first page,
 * IMPORTANT: which means the cache-freshness of this query is driven by the unread API.
 *
 * Follow these rules:
 *
 * 1. Call `checkUnread()` if you want to fetch latest in the background.
 * 2. Call `checkUnread({invalidate: true})` if you want latest to sync into this query's results immediately.
 * 3. Don't call this query's `refetch()` if you're trying to sync latest; call `checkUnread()` instead.
 */

runs.question: Is there an internal storybook or developer showcase screen in the app? Where is it defined?
runs.answer:
Yes. The app includes an internal **Storybook / developer‑showcase screen**.

* It is exposed as the `StorybookScreen` component, which renders a layout wrapper and lazily loads the actual storybook UI.  
  **Defined in:** `src/view/screens/Storybook/index.tsx` (lines 7‑24)【src/view/screens/Storybook/index.tsx#StorybookScreen】.

* The lazily‑loaded UI itself is the `Storybook` component, which sets theme preferences and renders the storybook UI.  
  **Defined in:** `src/view/screens/Storybook/Storybook.tsx` (lines 31‑160)【src/view/screens/Storybook/Storybook.tsx#Storybook】.
runs.confidence_rationale: 1 of 3 retrieved entities scored >=0.6 relevance; top match scored 0.72.

sources.name: StorybookScreen
sources.snippet:
export function StorybookScreen() {
  return (
    <Layout.Screen>
      <Layout.Header.Outer>
        <Layout.Header.BackButton />
        <Layout.Header.Content>
          <Layout.Header.TitleText>Storybook</Layout.Header.TitleText>
        </Layout.Header.Content>
        <Layout.Header.Slot />
      </Layout.Header.Outer>
      <Layout.Content keyboardShouldPersistTaps="handled">
        <Suspense fallback={null}>
          <Storybook />
        </Suspense>
      </Layout.Content>
    </Layout.Screen>
  )
}
sources.graph_context: Renders: Layout, Suspense (external), Storybook

sources.name: Storybook
sources.snippet:
export default function Storybook() {
  const {setColorMode, setDarkTheme} = useSetThemePrefs()
  const [showContainedList, setShowContainedList] = useState(false)
  const navigation = useNavigation<NavigationProp>()
  const requestDeviceGeolocation = useRequestDeviceGeolocation()
  const {setDeviceGeolocation} = useDeviceGeolocationApi()
  const inviteFriendsControl = Dialog.useDialogControl()

  return (
    <>
      <View style={[a.p_xl, a.gap_5xl, {paddingBottom: 100}]}>
        {!showContainedList ? (
          <>
            <View style={[a.flex_row, a.align_start, a.gap_md]}>
              <Button
                color="primary"
                size="small"
                label='Set theme to "system"'
                onPress={() => setColorMode('system')}>
                <ButtonText>System</ButtonText>
              </Button>
              <Button
                color="secondary"
                size="small"
                label='Set theme to "light"'
                onPress={() => setColorMode('light')}>
                <ButtonText>Light</ButtonText>
              </Button>
              <Button
                color="secondary"
                size="small"
                label='Set theme to "dim"'
                onPress={() => {
                  setColorMode('dark')
                  setDarkTheme('dim')
                }}>
                <ButtonText>Dim</ButtonText>
              </Button>
              <Button
                color="secondary"
                size="small"
                label='Set theme to "dark"'
                onPress={() => {
                  setColorMode('dark')
                  setDarkTheme('dark')
                }}>
                <ButtonText>Dark</ButtonText>
              </Button>
            </View>

            <Button
              color="primary"
              size="small"
              onPress={() => navigation.navigate('SharedPreferencesTester')}
              label="two"
              testID="sharedPrefsTestOp

sources.name: index.tsx
sources.snippet:
import {lazy, Suspense} from 'react'

import * as Layout from '#/components/Layout'

const Storybook = lazy(() => import('./Storybook'))

export function StorybookScreen() {
  return (
    <Layout.Screen>
      <Layout.Header.Outer>
        <Layout.Header.BackButton />
        <Layout.Header.Content>
          <Layout.Header.TitleText>Storybook</Layout.Header.TitleText>
        </Layout.Header.Content>
        <Layout.Header.Slot />
      </Layout.Header.Outer>
      <Layout.Content keyboardShouldPersistTaps="handled">
        <Suspense fallback={null}>
          <Storybook />
        </Suspense>
      </Layout.Content>
    </Layout.Screen>
  )
}
